hooks.command:
python -c "
import json, sys, os, subprocess
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
path = data.get('tool_input', {}).get('file_path', '') or data.get('tool_input', {}).get('path', '')
if not path.lower().endswith('skill.md'):
    sys.exit(0)
script = os.path.join(os.path.expandvars('%USERPROFILE%'), '.shared', 'friend', 'scripts', 'skill_consistency_check.py')
if not os.path.exists(script):
    sys.exit(0)
result = subprocess.run([sys.executable, script, '--side', 'both', '--skills-dir', os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(path))], capture_output=True, text=True)
if result.returncode != 0:
    out = {'decision': 'block', 'reason': 'skill-consistency-check FAIL:\n' + result.stdout[-2000:]}
    print(json.dumps(out))
    sys.exit(2)
"

usage_notes: Only fires when the file being edited ends with SKILL.md
Blocks (exit 2) only on FAIL-level findings; WARNs are non-blocking
Requires Python in PATH; on Windows uses the same python as the current process
To also block on warnings, add --strict to the command
